summary: electron-orbit impersonates OrbitDB and stages env dump plus obfuscated TLS beacon
details: The package presents itself as an Electron/OrbitDB-related component (name 'electron-orbit', author 'OrbitDB Archive', homepage pointing to the archived github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron repo) but its actual behavior is hostile to installers. (1) The postinstall script (package.json: "postinstall": "node install.js") writes the full process.env together with hostname and username into a file under bin/formatters/ and prepends ELF/MZ/Mach-O magic bytes so the staged env dump on disk looks like a native binary; an additional copy of process.env is written to install-log.json. (2) On require() of the main module, top-level code computes sha256(process.env.BuildType) and, when it matches a hardcoded digest, XOR-decodes a hidden URL and connects to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net:443 over a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client (custom buildClientHello/encryptRecord, raw net socket) that bypasses Node's https stack and certificate validation, fetching /icons/orbit.svg with a SAS token and discarding the response — a covert, env-gated check-in. The combination of name/author impersonation of a legitimate project, install-time staging of the installer's environment variables disguised as a native binary, and an obfuscated env-gated beacon over a custom TLS implementation is the structural shape of a targeted supply-chain attack against installers, not legitimate package behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
